10.reason: The loop condition stops before the last character, so it is never printed.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition stops before the last character, so it is never printed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: loop condition i<strlen(s)-1 stops before the last character, so the final character (e.g., 'F') is never printed. Change to i<strlen(s) and adjust logic to handle hyphens that may include the last character.

12.reason: [The condition 'a'<=s[i-1]&&s[i+1]<='z' does not ensure both characters are lowercase; it allows cases like s[i-1]='A' and s[i+1]='z', which violates the same-type requirement.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition checks if s[i-1] is >= 'a' but does not ensure s[i+1] is >= 'a'; similarly, it checks s[i+1] <= 'z' but not s[i-1] <= 'z'. This allows mixed cases like 'Z-a' to incorrectly pass if ASCII values satisfy s[i-1] < s[i+1]. Also, the same issue exists in uppercase and digit conditions.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition 'a'<=s[i-1]&&s[i+1]<='z' does not ensure both characters are lowercase; it allows cases like s[i-1]='A' and s[i+1]='z', which violates the same-type requirement.]

17.reason: [Similarly, 'A'<=s[i-1]&&s[i+1]<='Z' does not guarantee both are uppercase; e.g., s[i-1]='a' and s[i+1]='Z' would incorrectly pass.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: does not verify that s[i+1] >= 'A' or that s[i-1] <= 'Z', so invalid ranges like '9-A' might be misprocessed if ASCII comparison passes.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Similarly, 'A'<=s[i-1]&&s[i+1]<='Z' does not guarantee both are uppercase; e.g., s[i-1]='a' and s[i+1]='Z' would incorrectly pass.]

22.reason: [Same issue: '0'<=s[i-1]&&s[i+1]<='9' doesn't ensure both are digits; e.g., s[i-1]='a' and s[i+1]='9' would wrongly satisfy this.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: does not check that s[i+1] >= '0' or s[i-1] <= '9', allowing invalid digit-letter mixes to be processed if s[i-1] < s[i+1] in ASCII.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: '0'<=s[i-1]&&s[i+1]<='9' doesn't ensure both are digits; e.g., s[i-1]='a' and s[i+1]='9' would wrongly satisfy this.]

34.reason: [The last character of the input string is never printed because the loop runs from i=1 to strlen(s)-2 (inclusive). For input "a-uB-F", the final 'F' is skipped, causing missing output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The last character of the input string is never printed because the loop runs from i=1 to strlen(s)-2 (inclusive). For input "a-uB-F", the final 'F' is skipped, causing missing output.]
